sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: BHP share price tipped to rise to $56: expert
feed.summary: Amid soaring commodity prices, six brokers have re-evaluated their ratings and 12-month price targets for BHP Group Ltd (ASX: BHP). Bank of America's Jason Fairclough stands out, reaffirming a 'buy' rating and increasing his price target from $49 to $56, suggesting an 18% upside. Other analysts from Macquarie, Morgan Stanley, Citi, Barclays, and Ord Minnett also updated their targets, mostly reiterating 'hold' or 'buy' with price increases.

feed.title: Jefferies assumes coverage on Ulta Beauty stock with Hold rating By Investing.com
feed.summary: Jefferies has initiated coverage on Ulta Beauty (NASDAQ:ULTA) with a Hold rating and a $700 price target, noting the company's strong position in both prestige and mass beauty markets and its impressive 64.5% price return over the past year. The firm anticipates continued value-seeking consumer behavior and has raised Ulta's fourth-quarter and fiscal year 2026 comparable sales estimates due to improved product assortment and increased shopping frequency. Other analysts from Oppenheimer, Morgan Stanley, Evercore ISI, and BofA Securities have also recently adjusted their price targets for Ulta Beauty, generally maintaining positive outlooks.

feed.title: Fair Isaac stock price target raised to $1,981 from $1,914 at Clear Street
feed.summary: Clear Street raised its price target for Fair Isaac (NYSE:FICO) to $1,981 from $1,914, maintaining a Buy rating. The firm increased its earnings per share estimates for Q1 fiscal 2026, fiscal 2026, and fiscal 2027, citing stronger mortgage originations, lower operating expenses, and confidence in the company's Mortgage Direct License Program. Clear Street forecasts significant revenue and adjusted EPS growth, with key investor focus areas including program rollout, pricing, competition, and FICO Platform growth.

feed.title: Concrete Pumping Holdings beats Q4 estimates, but shares fall on outlook
feed.summary: Concrete Pumping Holdings (NASDAQ:BBCP) exceeded Q4 earnings and revenue estimates but saw its shares fall due to a cautious fiscal year 2026 outlook. The company's performance exhibited resilience despite a slowdown in construction markets attributed to high interest rates, with its U.S. Concrete Waste Management Services segment showing strong growth. For fiscal year 2026, the company anticipates revenue between $390.0 million and $410.0 million, assuming no significant recovery in the construction market.

feed.title: Halliburton names Casey Maxwell as Western Hemisphere president By Investing.com
feed.summary: Halliburton has appointed Casey Maxwell as the new president of Western Hemisphere operations, effective February 1, 2026. Maxwell, previously senior vice president of North America Land, will succeed Mark Richard, who is transitioning to a senior advisor role. The announcement comes as the energy services company trades near its 52-week high, with a strong financial position and continued dividend payments for 55 consecutive years.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Q4 revenue misses as net interest income rises less than expected (WFC:NYSE)
feed.summary: Wells Fargo (WFC) reported mixed Q4 results with revenue missing expectations due to lower-than-anticipated net interest income, which caused its stock to slip in premarket trading. The company provided 2026 net interest income guidance of approximately $50 billion, slightly below analyst consensus but an increase from 2025. Wells Fargo's growth is supported by loan and deposit increases, effective cost management, and strong consumer banking activity.

feed.title: China tells local companies to stop using US, Israeli cybersecurity software: report
feed.summary: China has reportedly ordered local companies to cease using cybersecurity software from about a dozen U.S. and Israeli firms due to national security concerns. This move is part of China's broader strategy to replace Western technology with domestic alternatives, fearing foreign software could collect and transmit confidential information abroad. The ban could affect major U.S. cybersecurity companies like Broadcom, Palo Alto Networks, Fortinet, and Check Point, impacting their business in the Chinese market.

feed.title: TCS, AMD Partner To Accelerate Enterprise AI Adoption At Scale
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) have formed a strategic partnership to accelerate enterprise AI adoption. The collaboration aims to help businesses transition AI initiatives from pilot to large-scale production, focusing on industry-specific AI and generative AI solutions. Both companies will co-invest in talent development and create frameworks for sectors like life sciences, manufacturing, and BFSI, leveraging AMD's high-performance computing and AI portfolio with TCS's expertise.

feed.title: Hormel Foods Announces Upcoming Retirements of Mark Morey and Paul Peil
feed.summary: Hormel Foods Corporation (NYSE: HRL) has announced the upcoming retirements of two long-standing leaders: Mark Morey, vice president of operations for fresh pork, and Paul Peil, vice president of marketing for fresh and ready meats. Interim CEO Jeff Ettinger praised Morey for his three decades of trusted leadership and contributions to the fresh pork business. The full story requires a login.

feed.title: Hasbro Inc.: Can a Classic Toy Powerhouse Reinvent Play for the Digital Generation?
feed.summary: Hasbro Inc. is transforming from a traditional toy company into a modern entertainment and play company, focusing on franchises that span physical products, digital experiences, and media tie-ins. Key to this strategy are brands like Dungeons & Dragons and Magic: The Gathering, which blend physical and digital play. The company faces stiff competition from other toy makers and digital entertainment platforms but leverages its deep IP portfolio, multi-generational appeal, and physical-digital hybrid products to drive growth.

feed.title: FCC kills Verizon's 60-day phone unlocking rule after massive fraud spike
feed.summary: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) has revoked a rule that mandated Verizon to unlock its mobile phones 60 days after activation. This decision follows Verizon's claims of widespread device fraud and trafficking networks, which cost the company hundreds of millions of dollars annually. The FCC concluded that the expedited unlocking period, intended to protect consumer choice, inadvertently created a loophole exploited by criminal networks for illicit resale, particularly in countries like Russia, China, and Cuba.
